description: Add unit tests for critical untested code paths identified during a systematic audit of the entire test surface. Each story adds tests for a specific module where complex logic has zero coverage, focusing on the highest-risk gaps: security-critical sanitization, tool dispatch pipeline, WebSocket lifecycle handlers, version checking, file watcher callbacks, build validation, and CORS protection.

userStories.title: Add unit tests for sanitizeOutput in mcp-setup.ts
userStories.description: As a developer, I want sanitizeOutput to have comprehensive unit tests so that prototype pollution prevention is verified and regressions are caught.
acceptanceCriteria: sanitizeOutput is exported from mcp-setup.ts (named export, not default)
Tests cover: flat object with __proto__/constructor/prototype keys stripped
Tests cover: nested objects with dangerous keys at multiple depths
Tests cover: arrays of objects with dangerous keys stripped
Tests cover: mixed arrays (primitives + objects) handled correctly
Tests cover: depth limit boundary — object nested 51+ levels returns raw value at depth 51
Tests cover: null, undefined, primitives (string, number, boolean) pass through unchanged
Tests cover: empty object returns empty object
Tests cover: keys that are partial matches (e.g., '__proto__x', 'constructorHelper') are NOT stripped
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
userStories.notes: The function is at platform/mcp-server/src/mcp-setup.ts lines 45-53. It is currently unexported — add a named export. The DANGEROUS_KEYS Set is defined at line 38: new Set(['__proto__', 'constructor', 'prototype']). Add tests to platform/mcp-server/src/mcp-setup.test.ts in a new describe('sanitizeOutput', ...) block. Follow the existing test patterns in that file (import at top, describe blocks with focused test cases). The depth limit is 50 — at depth 50 recursion continues, at depth 51 the raw value is returned. Test the boundary precisely. For the partial-match test, use keys like '__proto__x' and 'myConstructor' — these should survive sanitization since the check is `DANGEROUS_KEYS.has(key)` which is an exact-match Set lookup.

userStories.title: Add unit tests for tools/call handler dispatch pipeline in mcp-setup.ts
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the tools/call handler to have unit tests covering all 11 code paths so that concurrency management, error classification, and input validation bugs are caught.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests cover: browser tool Zod validation failure returns isError with formatted message
Tests cover: browser tool handler success returns sanitized output
Tests cover: browser tool handler throws returns 'Browser tool error: ...' message
Tests cover: plugin tool not found returns isError
Tests cover: plugin tool disabled returns isError
Tests cover: schema compilation failure (validate is null) returns descriptive error
Tests cover: validator throws (try-catch around validate call) returns 'validation failed unexpectedly'
Tests cover: validation failure returns 'Invalid arguments' with error details
Tests cover: concurrency limit exceeded returns 'Too many concurrent dispatches'
Tests cover: extension not connected returns 'Extension not connected'
Tests cover: dispatch success returns sanitized output
Tests cover: DispatchError with code -32001 prefixes 'Tab closed:'
Tests cover: DispatchError with code -32002 prefixes 'Tab unavailable:'
Tests cover: DispatchError with data.code (ToolError) prefixes '[CODE]'
Tests cover: generic dispatch error returns 'Tool dispatch error:'
Tests cover: activeDispatches counter decrements in finally block even on error
Tests cover: activeDispatches counter reaches 0 and entry is deleted from map
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
userStories.notes: The handler is at platform/mcp-server/src/mcp-setup.ts lines 206-403, inside registerMcpHandlers as a closure over CallToolRequestSchema. It is not directly exportable. The best approach is to test through the existing mock MCP server pattern already used in mcp-setup.test.ts — see how registerMcpHandlers is called with a mock server object. The mock server's setRequestHandler captures the handler function, which can then be called directly with mock requests. Key mock requirements: (1) state.cachedBrowserTools for browser tool path, (2) state.toolLookup Map with ToolLookupEntry objects (validate function, validationErrors function) for plugin tool paths, (3) state.activeDispatches Map for concurrency, (4) state.extensionWs for connection check, (5) mock dispatchToExtension for dispatch paths. The isDispatchError check (line 361) looks for { name: 'DispatchError', message, code } shape — use the same pattern from extension-protocol.test.ts. MAX_CONCURRENT_DISPATCHES_PER_PLUGIN is 5 (line 35). Add tests in platform/mcp-server/src/mcp-setup.test.ts in a new describe('tools/call handler', ...) block.

userStories.title: Add unit tests for wsClose and wsOpen handlers in http-routes.ts
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the WebSocket lifecycle handlers to have unit tests so that pending dispatch rejection and connection replacement ordering are verified.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests cover: wsClose with matching ws sets extensionWs to null
Tests cover: wsClose with matching ws rejects all pending dispatches with 'Extension disconnected'
Tests cover: wsClose clears timeout timers for all pending dispatches
Tests cover: wsClose with non-matching ws (stale close) does not modify state
Tests cover: wsClose with no pending dispatches is a no-op
Tests cover: wsOpen assigns new ws to state.extensionWs
Tests cover: wsOpen replaces previous ws (closes old one)
Tests cover: wsOpen assigns new ws BEFORE closing previous (ordering test)
Tests cover: wsOpen with no previous connection works without error
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
userStories.notes: The handlers are at platform/mcp-server/src/http-routes.ts lines 339-390. They are returned by createHandlers (line 415-423) which takes a RouteDeps object. createHandlers is already exported and used in tests — see http-routes.test.ts for the existing pattern with createMockState(). For wsClose tests: create a mock state with pendingDispatches containing entries with {reject, timerId} and verify reject is called with Error('Extension disconnected') and clearTimeout is called on timerId. For the stale-close test: set state.extensionWs to a different ws object, call wsClose with the old ws, verify state is unchanged. For wsOpen ordering test: mock the previous ws.close() to verify state.extensionWs already points to the new ws when close is called (use a mock that captures state inside the close callback). wsOpen also calls sendSyncFull which needs mocking — mock the extension-protocol module. Add tests in platform/mcp-server/src/http-routes.test.ts in new describe blocks.

userStories.title: Add unit tests for fetchLatestVersion and checkForUpdates in version-check.ts
userStories.description: As a developer, I want version checking functions to have unit tests so that scoped package URL encoding and update detection logic are verified.
acceptanceCriteria: fetchLatestVersion is exported from version-check.ts
Tests cover: unscoped package constructs correct npm registry URL
Tests cover: scoped package (@scope/pkg) encodes / as %2F in URL
Tests cover: successful fetch returns latest version string
Tests cover: non-200 response returns null
Tests cover: malformed JSON response returns null
Tests cover: network error (fetch throws) returns null
Tests cover: checkForUpdates filters out local plugins (no npmPackageName)
Tests cover: checkForUpdates populates state.outdatedPlugins for outdated npm plugins
Tests cover: checkForUpdates handles empty plugin list
Tests cover: checkForUpdates handles mixed settled results (some fulfilled, some rejected)
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
userStories.notes: fetchLatestVersion is at platform/mcp-server/src/version-check.ts lines 23-43. It is currently unexported — add a named export. checkForUpdates is at lines 80-124 and IS already exported. For testing, mock globalThis.fetch using Bun's mock module pattern or direct assignment. The scoped package URL is constructed as: `https://registry.npmjs.org/${encodeURIComponent(name)}/latest` — for @opentabs-dev/plugin-sdk this becomes @opentabs-dev%2Fplugin-sdk. The AbortSignal.timeout(5000) is used for fetch. For checkForUpdates: it reads from state.plugins Map, filters entries where p.npmPackageName is set, calls fetchLatestVersion for each, then uses isNewer to check. Add tests in platform/mcp-server/src/version-check.test.ts. Follow the existing describe/test pattern in that file.

userStories.title: Add unit tests for handleManifestChange in file-watcher.ts
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the file watcher manifest change handler to have unit tests so that manifest validation, name mismatch detection, and URL pattern filtering are verified.
acceptanceCriteria: handleManifestChange is exported from file-watcher.ts (or tested through an exported wrapper)
Tests cover: successful manifest update modifies plugin state (tools, urlPatterns, version, description, displayName)
Tests cover: file not found (Bun.file().text() throws) logs warning and does not crash
Tests cover: plugin name mismatch between manifest and expected name logs warning and skips
Tests cover: all URL patterns invalid after filtering results in warning and skips update
Tests cover: plugin not in state (stale callback) skips silently
Tests cover: IIFE re-read updates adapterHash in plugin state
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
userStories.notes: handleManifestChange is defined inside file-watcher.ts as a local function. It takes (pluginName, pluginDir, state, callbacks). To make it testable, export it as a named export. The function reads the manifest via Bun.file().text(), parses it with parseManifest(), validates URL patterns with validateUrlPattern, reads the IIFE for hash computation via Bun.file().text() on dist/adapter.iife.js, and calls callbacks.onManifestChanged. For testing: use real temp directories (following the pattern in file-watcher.test.ts and discovery-load.test.ts), write valid/invalid manifest files, and verify state mutations. The name mismatch check at ~line 135 strips 'opentabs-plugin-' prefix from the manifest name and compares with pluginName. Add tests in platform/mcp-server/src/file-watcher.test.ts in a new describe block.

userStories.description: As a developer, I want validatePlugin to have test coverage for displayName and icon validation so that invalid plugin metadata is caught at build time.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests cover: empty displayName string produces a validation error
Tests cover: valid displayName passes validation
Tests cover: missing displayName (undefined) passes validation (it's optional)
Tests cover: invalid icon name (not in LUCIDE_ICON_NAMES) produces a validation error
Tests cover: valid icon name passes validation
Tests cover: tool with empty displayName produces a validation error
Tests cover: tool with valid displayName passes validation

bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
userStories.notes: Add to platform/cli/src/commands/build.test.ts in the existing describe('validatePlugin', ...) block. The plugin displayName check is at build.ts line 34: `if (plugin.displayName !== undefined && plugin.displayName.length === 0)`. The tool icon check is at build.ts ~line 61: validates against LUCIDE_ICON_NAMES Set. The tool displayName check is at ~line 55. Use the existing makePlugin() and makeTool() helpers. For icon testing, use a known valid icon like 'wrench' (already used in makeTool defaults) and an invalid one like 'nonexistent-icon-name'. Follow the existing nested describe pattern (e.g., describe('displayName', () => ...) and describe('icon', () => ...)).

userStories.title: Add CORS protection tests to http-routes.test.ts
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the CORS protection to have unit tests so that DNS rebinding attack prevention is verified and cannot regress.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests cover: request with Origin: http://evil.com returns 403 Forbidden
Tests cover: request with Origin: https://attacker.io returns 403 Forbidden
Tests cover: request with Origin: chrome-extension://abc123 passes through (not rejected)
Tests cover: request with no Origin header passes through (not rejected)
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
userStories.notes: The CORS check is at platform/mcp-server/src/http-routes.ts lines 109-112. It runs early in the fetch handler (before route matching). Test by calling the fetch handler returned by createHandlers with a Request that has an Origin header set. For the 'passes through' tests, use a known route like /health to verify the request reaches the route handler (returns 200) instead of being blocked by CORS (403). Follow the existing test patterns in http-routes.test.ts — the mock state and bunServer patterns are already established. Add a new describe('CORS protection', ...) block.

userStories.title: Add E2E test for browser_handle_dialog with actual dialog
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an E2E test that exercises browser_handle_dialog with a real browser dialog so that the full dialog handling pipeline is verified end-to-end.
acceptanceCriteria: Test navigates to the interactive test page which has a #show-alert button
Test triggers a dialog by clicking the alert button or executing window.alert() via browser_execute_script
Test calls browser_handle_dialog with accept action and verifies success
Test verifies the dialog was actually dismissed (page is interactive again)
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
bun run test:e2e passes
userStories.notes: The test server at e2e/test-server.ts already serves an /interactive page with a #show-alert button (check the HTML served for /interactive). The browser_handle_dialog tool uses the Chrome DevTools Protocol (chrome.debugger.attach + Page.javascriptDialogOpening). The test should: (1) open a tab to the test app, (2) use browser_execute_script to run `window.alert('test dialog')` — but note that alert() is blocking, so the execute_script call will hang until the dialog is dismissed. Instead, schedule the alert with setTimeout: `setTimeout(() => window.alert('test'), 100)`, then immediately call browser_handle_dialog. Alternatively, trigger via the #show-alert button click using browser_click_element, then call browser_handle_dialog. Add to e2e/browser-tools.e2e.ts in the existing 'dialog handling' describe block. Look at how the existing 'no dialog open' test works and follow the same pattern for the success case. The existing test at browser-tools.e2e.ts already has a describe('dialog handling', ...) block — add the new test there.

userStories.title: Add unit tests for checkForUpdates filtering and state population
userStories.description: As a developer, I want checkForUpdates orchestration to have unit tests so that local plugin filtering and outdatedPlugins map population are verified.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests cover: local plugins without npmPackageName are skipped (not checked against registry)
Tests cover: npm plugins with npmPackageName are checked
Tests cover: outdated plugin (isNewer returns true) is added to state.outdatedPlugins
Tests cover: up-to-date plugin is NOT added to state.outdatedPlugins
Tests cover: fetchLatestVersion returning null (network failure) does not add to outdatedPlugins
Tests cover: empty plugins map results in empty outdatedPlugins
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
userStories.notes: checkForUpdates is already exported from platform/mcp-server/src/version-check.ts. It takes a ServerState object. It iterates state.plugins, filters for entries with npmPackageName set, calls fetchLatestVersion for each, and uses isNewer to decide whether to add to state.outdatedPlugins. To test: create a mock state with plugins Map, mock globalThis.fetch to return controlled responses. This story can share the fetch mock setup from US-004. Add tests in platform/mcp-server/src/version-check.test.ts. Note: this story is intentionally separate from US-004 (fetchLatestVersion) to keep each story focused. If US-004 already exports fetchLatestVersion and mocks fetch, this story can build on that. However, each story must be independently completable — so include the fetch mock setup in this story's tests even if US-004 also does it.
